title: Validation layer has NO cross-field consistency check between derived.status and canonical completion signals; --strict is structurally blind to false-complete (detection-side gap that let D4-P0-001's 213+ false-completes pass undetected)
claim: every validator treats derived.status as isolated enum (integrity) or ignores it (drift/continuity-freshness/spec-doc-structure/evidence-marker-lint); none cross-checks stored complete against completion_pct/open-tasks/checklist-content; false complete passes --strict whenever enum-valid + parseable

NO (within-canonical-doc only); grep miss a validator outside validation/? chain is closed set of 5 (validate.sh:18-30) all read/grep-confirmed; orchestrator cross-ref? grep-confirmed no
alternativeExplanation: blindness may be by-design: validator trusts producer derivation, defers semantic is-done to canonical-doc checks (continuity-freshness reads source-of-truth frontmatter not derived field); defensible single-layer design but leaves producer bug undetectable at the only per-commit gate
downgradeTrigger: cross-field status-vs-completion_pct-vs-open-tasks validator added to --strict chain OR design doc establishes canonical-doc-only trust boundary as contractual and accepts producer-bug-latency tradeoff
relationship: distinct from D1-P2-001 (format axis) and D4-P0-001 (producer axis); FORMALIZES iter-6 embedded D4-P0-001.blastRadiusAssessment.consumerMap.validatorBlind into standalone actionable finding
